Agree. The introduction of additional trauma through tooth extraction during an acute infection indeed places greater strain on the immune system, potentially leading to wider distribution of bacteria into the bloodstream. Furthermore, while antibiotics may partially address this issue, they cannot entirely prevent the risk of sepsis; therefore, postponing elective extractions until the infection resolves minimizes these adverse effects.
